name: Kedgeree
recipeIngredient: 2 naturally smoked haddock fillets, approximately 150 grams each
280 grams of Veetee Dine-in basmati rice (or 150 grams of plain basmati rice)
2 tablespoons crème fraîche or thick cream
recipeInstructions:
1. If cooking the rice from scratch, wash it until the water runs clear and place in a saucepan with cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to as low as possible and cook for 10 minutes. Remove from heat and let stand without removing the lid for an additional 10 minutes.
2. Boil eggs in a small pan of water for 9 minutes. Drain, crack the eggs over running cold water, and peel.
3. Place haddock fillets in a large frying pan (or any pan that can hold them in a single layer). Add 300 milliliters of cold water. Simmer for 5-6 minutes until cooked through. Strain cooking liquid into a jug. Allow fish to cool.
4. Peel and finely chop the onion. Melt butter in a small pan, then gently soften the onion. Stir in cumin, turmeric, chili, and curry powder, frying for a couple of minutes before stirring in flour. Remove from heat and gradually stir in strained-fishing water using a whisk to ensure a smooth, thick sauce. Simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Cook Veetee Dine-in rice according to packet instructions (stir-fried or microwaved for 2 minutes).
6. Stir crème fraîche into the sauce and remove from heat. Flake fish directly into the sauce. Divide cooked rice onto two warmed plates (or one large platter) and spoon fishy sauce over top.
7. Arrange quartered eggs on top of the rice. Scatter coarsely chopped coriander leaves over the dish.
8. Serve with lime wedges.
